Missed multi-label: removal of the 2 hydrocarbon tanks is itself a mise en sécurité action under R512-39-1 (évacuation des produits dangereux), matching M19_CESSATION alongside the conformity confirmation (M08), consistent with sibling records f01/f02/f06/f07 in the same report.

M09_PAC content (dossier de demande d'autorisation jugé non recevable) is a purely administrative/procedural gap with no physical dimension stated in constats_body -> G2, consistent with every other M09_PAC record in this batch (all tagged G2).

error_patterns: Two dominant, batch-specific patterns account for 40 of the 57 corrections. (1) STRICT code-validity: hunter-44 never once populated the `modifiers` array — whenever it detected a délai it stuffed 'm_DELAI' inside `mechanisms` instead (9 records), and once leaked a dynamic-axis code ('R09') into `mechanisms` (1 record). (2) The flagged S02_INJONCTION/m_DELAI pairing check: Hunter under-used both m_DELAI and S02_INJONCTION together — 10 records already staged S02 were missing the m_DELAI modifier despite an explicit numeric deadline in the demande, and a further 9 records carried a formal 'Demande à formuler'/'Observations' section with an explicit numeric délai but were left at S01_CONSTAT instead of promoted to S02_INJONCTION (all fixed, with m_DELAI added alongside). Conversely, 3 records had m_DELAI attached to demandes with only 'dès réception' (no number) and had it removed. Beyond the délai/stage cluster, the recurring qualitative error was False M08/M14 confusion on nomenclature-threshold constats: 3 records about determining whether an installation is subject to ICPE classification ('ne relève pas de', 'non classé') were tagged M08_CONFORMITE or M14_CONSTAT_TECHNIQUE instead of M17_CLASSIFICATION. One record (le-pot-au-pin f04, 2024-05-28) was a genuine False M08: the inspector explicitly could not verify most of the installation and surfaced a new physical degradation (staircase), requiring M08->M14, G1->G3, R13->R14 and T3->T2 together. One missed multi-label (M19 alongside M08) and one gravity outlier inconsistent with sibling M09_PAC records in the same batch (G3->G2) round out the findings. The two systematic patterns explicitly flagged for this audit (R10_TENSION misuse and S02/m_DELAI mismatch) were checked batch-wide: R10 does not appear anywhere in hunter-44's dynamic field (no instances to correct), while the S02/m_DELAI mismatch was confirmed as a real, substantial issue as described above. All 130 records carried empty `reasoning` strings from Hunter; reasoning was written for every corrected record in final-44.json and left as Hunter supplied (empty) for uncorrected records, since reasoning content is outside the taxonomy-code audit scope.
verdict: Below the batch's usual quality bar: hunter-44 has a genuine, batch-wide STRICT-validity defect (modifiers array never used) and a systematic under-application of the S02_INJONCTION/m_DELAI pairing, together accounting for 70% of all corrections. Once these mechanical defects are fixed, the underlying mechanism/gravity/trajectory/dynamic judgment calls were mostly sound — only 6 of 130 records needed a substantive re-classification (M08/M14 vs M17, one multi-label miss, one gravity outlier). No instances of R10_TENSION misuse, no invented/mangled codes elsewhere in the batch, and confidence was uniformly (and appropriately, in retrospect) 'high' since none of the found errors were genuinely ambiguous once the corpus was re-read. final-44.json validates cleanly against scripts/tag_status.py.
